completeness.rationale: Extracted ~5,000 tasks, description vs title search distinction, v2.2→v2.3 regression, ~2 weeks timing, and 10-15s latency. Missed CPU spike detail and long descriptions, but captured the most critical items.

accuracy.rationale: Root cause hypothesis directly names the switch from indexed/full-text search to naive LIKE/full-table scan, which is exactly the actual cause. The divergence between title and description search paths is correctly identified. No inaccuracies.

efficiency.rationale: Only 1 question turn was needed to extract nearly all critical information. The multi-choice format efficiently covered both timing/trigger and search pattern in a single message. No wasted questions.

question_quality.rationale: The structured multiple-choice question about timing was excellent — it guided the reporter to connect the upgrade to the regression. The follow-up about whether all searches or specific queries were slow directly elicited the critical title-vs-description distinction the reporter hadn't initially volunteered.

actionability.rationale: The summary provides a precise root cause hypothesis, clear reproduction steps, a recommended fix pointing exactly where to look (diff search paths between versions, check for removed FTS index), and even a proposed regression test. A developer could start investigating immediately.

notable_strengths: Extracted the critical title-vs-description distinction in a single turn with a well-crafted question
Root cause hypothesis is remarkably accurate, correctly identifying FTS-to-LIKE regression
Excellent actionability — reproduction steps, fix direction, and test case are all concrete and correct
Multiple-choice format reduced friction for the reporter while covering multiple diagnostic angles
notable_weaknesses: Did not discover CPU spike behavior during searches
Did not ask about task description length/content, which is relevant to the full-table scan performance
Environment details are thin — OS and hardware specs not captured despite being mentioned in ground truth
most_insightful_question: Also — is every search slow, or only certain queries (e.g., longer search terms, specific filters)?
missed_opportunities: Could have asked about system resource usage (CPU/memory) during slow searches to further confirm full-table scan hypothesis
Could have asked about description content/length to understand data characteristics
Could have asked whether the reporter tried any workarounds like clearing cache or reindexing
